features.summary: Nike reported Q3 fiscal 2021 results with 3% revenue growth (+1% currency-neutral decline) driven by 16% NIKE Direct growth and 54% digital growth, offset by wholesale declines due to supply chain disruptions. North America revenue declined 11% due to container shortages and port congestion delaying inventory by 3+ weeks. Greater China was the standout with 42% growth, while EMEA declined 9% due to COVID lockdowns. Gross margin expanded 130 basis points from higher full-price sales and favorable digital mix. Digital now represents over 35% of total business, with member buying growth of 80% year-over-year.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year revenue guidance to low-to-mid teens growth and expects Q4 revenue growth of roughly 75% as supply chain issues normalize and European lockdowns ease. They anticipate gross margin expansion up to 75 basis points for the full year and expect digital to continue as the fastest-growing channel toward their 50% digital vision. The company is confident in strong free cash flow growth and will resume moderate share repurchases in Q4. Management expressed optimism about fiscal 2022 momentum, noting they're already exceeding pre-pandemic business levels.
features.risks: Primary risks include ongoing global supply chain disruptions with container shortages and port congestion extending inventory transit times by 3+ weeks, impacting wholesale shipment timing. COVID-related lockdowns continue affecting physical retail, with 35% of NIKE-owned stores in EMEA currently closed. Foreign exchange headwinds are estimated at 35 basis points for the full year. The company faces higher logistics and freight costs, and potential for increased markdowns to clear excess inventory in EMEA as lockdowns ease.
features.key_drivers: Digital transformation remains the primary growth driver with 54% digital growth and over 60% increase in monthly engaged users. Greater China delivered exceptional 42% growth with strong brand momentum across all categories. Women's business showed particular strength with nearly 90% digital growth and high double-digit retail growth. Jordan Brand grew 15% with triple-digit women's growth. The consumer direct acceleration strategy is driving margin expansion through higher-margin digital sales and improved pricing analytics, while member acquisition and retention continue to outpace overall growth.
